title: Triniochloa micrantha
taxonomy: Triniochloa micrantha is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae and the genus Triniochloa. This classification places it within the subfamily Chloridoideae. Triniochloa micrantha is further categorized as a member of the tribe Cynodoceae. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Triniochloa micrantha is a complex system that helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
constituents: Triniochloa micrantha is a plant species that is found in various regions, but information about its active constituents is limited. There is a lack of research and data available on the specific compounds and their effects on the plant. More studies are needed to identify and understand the active constituents of Triniochloa micrantha. However, general knowledge suggests that plants in the Poaceae family often contain compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, but this is not specific to Triniochloa micrantha. Further research is required to determine the exact active constituents of this plant.
